text: A 54-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting. Labs reveal markedly elevated serum lipase and amylase.

A 29-year-old woman presents with intermittent palpitations, heat intolerance, and unintentional weight loss. Physical exam reveals a fine tremor, tachycardia, and a diffusely enlarged, non-tender thyroid gland.
A 7-year-old boy with a 3-week history of periorbital edema and frothy urine. Urinalysis shows heavy proteinuria without hematuria, and serum albumin is low.
A 68-year-old man with a long smoking history presents with painless gross hematuria. CT urography reveals a mass in the bladder wall.
A 34-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of severe unilateral throbbing headache preceded by visual scotomata, accompanied by photophobia, nausea, and phonophobia.
A 45-year-old man with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes presents with fever, facial pain, black necrotic eschar on the nasal mucosa, and periorbital swelling.

A 3-month-old infant with projectile non-bilious vomiting after feeding, visible peristalsis, and a palpable olive-shaped mass in the epigastrium. Labs show hypochloremic, hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
A 60-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, dry cough, and clubbing of the fingers. Chest CT reveals bilateral basal reticular opacities with honeycombing.
A 19-year-old male presents with fever, sore throat, fatigue, and posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Physical exam reveals splenomegaly, and a monospot test is positive.
A 73-year-old man with sudden onset of a painless, curtain-like loss of vision in the right eye, preceded by flashes of light and floaters.
